svg.text: ESP32-Based Smart Plug Energy Monitoring and Appliance Control System
A. AC MAINS AND LOAD SIDE - HIGH VOLTAGE AREA
Mains wiring must be enclosed and fused

text.tspan: AC L and AC N input

svg.text: CT around one live wire only

text.tspan: Add fuse on live input before relay, HLK, and PZEM.
Use proper wire gauge, insulation, and strain relief.
Do not work on the circuit while energized.
Voltage terminals are before relay for stable voltage telemetry.
CT clamp goes only around the relay-output live wire.
Do not clamp live and neutral together.
Use COM + NO for normal smart-plug behavior.
If wired to NC, the appliance can stay powered while app says OFF.
Neutral goes directly to load neutral.

svg.text: B. LOW-VOLTAGE CONTROL AND MODULE CONNECTIONS
All GND pins are common; net labels mean connected together

text.tspan: HLK +5V -> ESP32 VIN/5V, relay VCC, SD VCC, PZEM VCC, DFPlayer VCC
HLK GND and every module GND -> common GND
GPIO27 <- Pairing reset button to GND
Green LED: GPIO2 -> 220-330 ohm -> LED -> GND

svg.text: Verify real module pin labels before soldering or powering mains.
